title: EoE gravity pull
wikitext:
[]: EoE: gravity pull             Quote Post by qu4d » Wed May 01, 2013 3:53 pm Just out of curiosity and I know, wikipedia isn't the best source, which is the reason why I'm asking. Wikipedia mentions: Lilith/Rei looks to the sky, pulled by the Earth's gravity, and cradles the Egg of Lilith which absorbed the souls of all human beings.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed May 01, 2013 4:03 pm Yeah, I know, an anime occasionally obeying the laws of physics from time to time, ridiculous, right?

[]:             Quote Post by Elect G-Max » Wed May 01, 2013 4:03 pm We do see giant chunks of Rei falling to the Earth at the end... I would assume that gravity has some effect on her.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed May 01, 2013 4:03 pm Her hair tends to hang in the direction of Earth. It's sort of an odd thing to mention in a summary, though.

[]:             Quote Post by qu4d » Wed May 01, 2013 4:16 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Yeah, I know, an anime occasionally obeying the laws of physics from time to time, ridiculous, right?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed May 01, 2013 4:51 pm The laws of physics and frankly geometry are frequently broken in EoE. It's a difficult film to follow locale-wise, let alone plot-wise. This is something someone has just added to the Wikipedia page for some reason.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed May 01, 2013 6:00 pm Lilith-Rei is God. Her hair isn't flopping all over the place because she doesn't want it to. We're talking about a creature that, by the square-cube rule, should explode, who passes though solid objects and creates the equivalent of about a quarter of the Earth's mass out of thin air. It's like asking how Jesus could ascend after meeting the apostles on the road, I mean why doesn't gravity keep him down?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu May 02, 2013 11:01 am I think it's referring to how she first stands up to full height, briefly appears to look at the sky, and then starts to hang forward/ gets into a crouching sort of position. She can obviously ignore gravity to a certain degree since she isn't collapsing unter her own weight, but maybe she's just needed a little getting-used-to her new proportions? Depending on how you see it, she used to either be a whole lot lighter, or have a different body shape.

[]:             Quote Post by VirtualBathsalt » Fri May 03, 2013 11:04 pm I think this comes down to 2 seperate assumptions: Maybe she's born with it. or Maybe it's Maybelline. I'd imagine the description that started the discussion was inferred, but it's one you can infer from simple observation. Like Reichu said, her hair (and other features (I mean boobs)) show an attraction to earth beneath her.
